headline: DaVinci Resolve brings professional color grading to still photography
lede: Blackmagic Design integrates its flagship node-based color science and AI-powered VFX tools into the photographic pipeline. Photographers can now manage native RAW files and tethered shooting within a singular, high-performance production environment.

headline: Backblaze silently excludes cloud storage folders from automatic backups
lede: Users report that Backblaze has quietly updated its software to ignore OneDrive, Dropbox, and hidden directories without explicit notification. This policy change forces a re-evaluation of what constitutes a genuine, off-site data backup.

headline: Google targets back button hijacking with new search spam policies
lede: Deceptive sites that manipulate browser history to trap users will face automated demotions starting this June. The update seeks to restore predictable navigation across the web by classifying history-hijacking as a malicious dark pattern.

headline: Thirty WordPress plugins compromised via coordinated investor backdoor scheme
lede: A malicious actor purchased established plugins solely to inject dormant code controlled by Ethereum smart contracts. The breach underscores the systemic fragility of relying on unverified third-party dependencies in modern web development.

headline: Introspective diffusion models aim to accelerate token generation
lede: New research introduces Introspective Strided Decoding to bypass the traditional sequential bottlenecks of autoregressive language models. By verifying tokens mid-pass, this method attempts to reconcile parallel processing with high-performance output standards.

headline: GitHub introduces native stacked pull request support
lede: The new gh stack CLI tool enables atomic management of sequential changes, allowing for cascading rebases and combined merges. This addition aims to reduce the friction inherent in large-scale branch protection and dependency workflows.

headline: OpenDuck enables distributed execution for DuckDB instances
lede: By reimplementing differential storage and remote execution protocols, this project allows DuckDB to function across decentralized backends. It provides a self-hosted alternative to proprietary cloud-based analytical engines.

headline: The legal war between Apple and its Franklin clones
lede: In the early 1980s, the Franklin ACE series brought Apple II compatibility to the market through bold hardware and firmware replication. A landmark appellate ruling eventually halted the clones, forever altering the landscape of software copyright law.

headline: Ransomware activity growth is outpacing security investment
lede: New industry data reveals that while security spending rises at a steady clip, the frequency of ransomware leak-site claims has accelerated significantly. The widening gap between defensive budgets and threat volume marks a concerning shift in corporate risk management.

headline: Hidden vulnerabilities persist in verified codebases
lede: Formal proofs are often limited by the scope of their underlying specifications and the trustworthiness of the runtime. When fuzzing a verified zlib implementation, researchers discovered that high-level correctness does not preclude failures in the trusted computing base.
pullquote: Formal verification isolates logical errors but cannot substitute for a holistic view of system integrity
